title: Cyclosorus marquesicus
intro: Cyclosorus marquesicus is a species of fern, specifically a type of tree fern, belonging to the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus marquesicus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Cyclosorus marquesicus is used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Cyclosorus marquesicus is classified in the family Thelypteridaceae, a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or autumn ferns. This classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other species within the family. Further classification places it in the genus Cyclosorus, a group of ferns primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. The family Thelypteridaceae is part of the larger order Polypodiales, which includes many species of ferns. Understanding the classification of Cyclosorus marquesicus helps scientists to better comprehend its behavior, habitat, and potential conservation status.

parts: Cyclosorus marquesicus is a species of fern native to the island of Marquesas. The medicinal parts of Cyclosorus marquesicus include its rhizomes and leaves, which are traditionally used in various folk remedies. The rhizomes are believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties and are used to treat various health conditions. The leaves of the plant are also used to treat fever and respiratory issues.
